items.title: Church ordains man facing rape charge interstate as a deacon

children.content: A man who is facing a rape charge in Queensland — which he has pleaded not guilty to — has been appointed to a leadership role in a Tasmanian Seventh-day Adventist church.

gallery.altText: A man with a long red beard stands with his hands clasped in a church while another man with a mic puts a hand on his shoulder.

thumbnail.altText: A man with a long red beard stands with his hands clasped in a church while another man with a mic puts a hand on his shoulder.

items.title: It was a record race time. Moments later spectators were suspicious

children.content: Tasmania's top horse race was run 37 metres shorter than advertised. Officials knew the track distance was likely incorrect but proceeded anyway. This is what happened.

image.altText: Elevated view of horse racing event with crowd looking on.

items.title: Three Tasmanian police employees charged over separate incidents

children.content: Three Tasmanian Police employees have been charged over separate incidents in the space of six days.

image.altText: Unidentified Tasmania Police officers in parade uniform standing in line.

items.title: Traffic gridlock as park visitors turn out to see the turning of the fagus

children.content: Nature lovers who went to see the autumn colours of the fagus at Tasmania's Mt Field National Park on Saturday say there were no car spaces and nowhere to turn around.

image.altText: Two rows of cars trying to pass on a narrow dirt road.

items.title: China accused of trying to 'bankrupt' Hobart, Port Pirie Nyrstar smelters

children.content: SA's treasurer and energy minister says China is undermining Australia's smelting industry and that any shutdown of Nyrstar's smelters would leave national security "exposed".

image.altText: An industrial site on the edge of a river, as seen from above.

items.title: Housing provider blamed tenant for soaked carpet, then regulator stepped in

children.content: Despite unsafe and unusable bathrooms, cockroach infestations eating through wiring and constantly damp carpets, some Tasmanian public housing tenants are having to get the regulator to intervene in order to get solutions.

images.altText: An Emirati patrol boat is near a tanker anchored in the Gulf of Oman near the Strait of Hormuz.

children.content: A large fire has also broken out at the key UAE oil port Fujairah, after it was hit by an Iranian drone attack. Follow live.

items.title: US sinks multiple Iranian boats, UAE intercepts missiles for first time since ceasefire

updates.title: Trump issues fresh warning to Iran
Trump says small boats are all Iran has left
US admiral says six Iranian small boats destroyed

items.title: Capital gains tax, negative gearing and trusts to form budget tax trio

children.content: The government is still finalising the details of its budget, but several sources say three tax policies that featured in Labor's 2019 election campaign will be revived and rethought.

image.altText: Jim Chalmers Anthony Albanese and Katy Gallagher__4924102b288b023a6a758034ed96da4a

items.title: Reserve Bank faces a choice between two evils: recession or inflation

children.content: If there's any certainty about the RBA's interest rate verdict, it's that it will be a split decision.

image.altText: The exterior of a concrete building marked with Reserve Bank of Australia, hedges in the foreground and people walking past.

items.title: Overcharging, 'kidnapping' and identity theft rife amid NDIS rorting

children.content: Insiders and participants say the NDIS is being targeted in similar ways around the country, including billing for services not provided, overcharging and in some cases "kidnapping" of vulnerable clients in order to park them in expensive supported accommodation and drain their plans.

image.altText: The NDIS helps Cooper get to school, out in the community and with daily life tasks

items.title: Electric vehicle tax discount to be wound back from next year

children.content: A popular tax discount meaning people leasing electric vehicles did not have to pay Fringe Benefits Tax will be wound back — although in the long term, more EV owners will be able to pay a reduced tax rate.

image.altText: People stand around a car with all its doors open with a sign in beg letters on the wall behind reading BYD.

items.title: Warning of 'tsunami of chronic disease' as dialysis units ration care

children.content: Dialysis units across Australia and New Zealand are being forced to ration lifesaving care for patients with kidney failure, according to a major new report which has warned of a "significant threat" to patient safety.

gallery.altText: Sarah Latham in a hospital bed with a birthday cake in front of her.

thumbnail.altText: Sarah Latham in a hospital bed with a birthday cake in front of her.
A patient's hand hooked up to a dialysis machine.

items.title: Sydney Lord Mayor Clover Moore cancels 'globalise the intifada' forum

children.content: The event's cancellation is welcomed by groups representing the Jewish community in New South Wales.

image.altText: A woman wearing red sunglasses

items.title: World order could be 'rebuilt out of Europe', Carney says

children.content: The Canadian prime minister tells European leaders the world does not need to submit to a "more transactional and insular world".

image.altText: A close up of Mark Carney speaking at a press conference.

items.title: 'He's a big boy': Socceroos boss defends anti-Trump comments from midfielder

children.content: Australia coach Tony Popovic says it would be "wonderful" if Donald Trump was in the stadium in Seattle when the Socceroos played the United States on June 19, saying the US president's presence would motivate his team.

image.altText: US President Donald Trump holds the FIFA World Cup Trophy

items.title: Two dead as car hits pedestrians in German city of Leipzig

children.content: Authorities say two people are dead after a car slammed into a crowd of people in the eastern German city.

image.altText: Police tape rolled out in front a police van in a large European city square.

items.title: How did this boy from the bush become head of the highest court in the land?

children.content: The High Court makes decisions that impact us every day. So who is Stephen Gageler, the person overseeing it all? And how did he get here from a tiny town in regional NSW?

gallery.altText: Leaning over Dennis, who is looking at a book, the chief justice smiles at the camera, a bookcase lined with books behind them.
The Chief Justice sits on  his desk before a series of bookcases lined with books.

thumbnail.altText: Leaning over Dennis, who is looking at a book, the chief justice smiles at the camera, a bookcase lined with books behind them.
The Chief Justice sits on  his desk before a series of bookcases lined with books.

items.title: Cruise ship banned from port after three die in suspected virus outbreak

children.content: Three people have died and three people are ill after a suspected outbreak of the rodent-borne virus in the Atlantic Ocean.

image.altText: MV Hondius photo

items.title: Australia and Japan sign agreements on energy, defence and critical minerals

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese and his Japanese counterpart Takaichi Sanae agreed to bolster cooperation on energy, defence and critical minerals, while warning against economic coercion.

image.altText: a female politician wearing a blazer at a table

items.title: HRT shortages prompt women to cut patches in half, travel interstate

children.content: Supplies of some HRT patches may not return to normal until at least the end of the year. So what can patients do if they're impacted by shortages?

gallery.altText: An unidentified woman applies a patch under her shirt to her abdomen.

thumbnail.altText: An unidentified woman applies a patch under her shirt to her abdomen.

items.title: Met Gala quiz: Who Am I? Guess who wore these outfits to fashion's best party

children.content: From Ariana to Zendaya, we're testing your knowledge of some of the most exceptional and extreme fashion on show at recent Met Galas.

image.altText: Doja Cat poses at the Met Gala, an annual fundraising gala, dressed as a cat.

items.title: Quiz: Can you name the typo on the $50 note?

children.content: Take a trip back in time with this week's history quiz.

image.altText: A shot of the $50 Australian note with a thumb holding it.

items.title: Sports broadcaster Dennis Cometti farewelled at state memorial service

children.content: A host of high-profile Australians gather at Perth Stadium to pay tribute to one of the nation's most well-known sports broadcasters, Dennis Cometti.

gallery.altText: A yellow jacket and a framed picture of Dennis Cometti on a stage in Perth.

thumbnail.altText: A yellow jacket and a framed picture of Dennis Cometti on a stage in Perth.

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby remembered across NT after 'unthinkable trauma'

children.content: The Northern Territory is continuing to grieve the short life of a five-year-old girl today with crowds in Alice Springs to pay tribute by donning pink, her favourite colour.

image.altText: Two people standing in the water, holding one another with their eyes closed.

items.title: The treasurer's reality check on Anthony Albanese's bold childcare plan

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has previously committed to delivering universal child care, but Treasurer Jim Chalmers has highlighted concerns about the cost.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese sits at a table talking with children in a child care class

items.title: ABC staff accept enterprise agreement after pay dispute strike

children.content: ABC staff have struck an enterprise agreement with the broadcaster, bringing an end to a pay dispute that culminated in a 24-hour strike.

image.altText: People strike and hold up signs.

items.title: Russian woman's husband beat her but police were no help, so she wrote to Putin

children.content: Domestic violence and abuse have been described as an "epidemic" in multiple countries, but analysts argue there is something that sets Russia apart: the government is making things worse.

image.altText: Close up of a woman who bears the results of being assaulted, with one eye partly closed.

items.title: Officers outraged after investigation uncovers taser security flaw

children.content: Police associations and unions demand urgent action over revelations that a security flaw allows officers to be tracked by criminals with a phone or laptop.

articles.title: A hacker built a tool to track police officers. Then he tried to warn them

children.content: A security flaw means the location of devices used by police, potentially including undercover officers and those travelling home, is visible to anyone with a phone or laptop.

image.altText: A man wearing a dark hoodie is silhouetted against the lights of a police station with police cars in front.

gallery.altText: A police officer, wearing a tactical vest with various pieces of equipment including a taser, looks the phone in his hands.

thumbnail.altText: A police officer, wearing a tactical vest with various pieces of equipment including a taser, looks the phone in his hands.

avatar.alt: A headshot of a woman with short blonde hair

items.title: 'Motivation to swim faster': Four people bitten by crocodile before swimming race

children.content: Four people were bitten by a suspected freshwater crocodile just a day before a sold-out swimming competition was run in the same far-north Western Australia lake.

image.altText: A freshwater crocodile resting next to a lake

items.title: AFL umpire Nick Foot's betting agency role over after tribunal case

children.content: Betting agency Sportsbet announces it will no longer feature current sports administrators or officials in their programming "following feedback".

image.altText: AFL umpire, Nick Foot, bends down and tenses before throwing the ball in to restart play during a game.

items.title: Daughter hopes $1m reward will solve mystery of mother's disappearance

children.content: Marion Barter has not been seen since 1997. Her daughter is determined to find out what happened to her mother, who she believes may have found herself "in a situation she did not fully control".

image.altText: Woman with dark hair poses with two young children by her side
